Numerous  tests  have  been  made  with  different  kinds  of  wood  to 
ascertain  how  long  one  pound  of  each  would,  in  comhuslion,  main- 
tain ten  degrees  of  heal  in  Ihc  same  room.  These  ranged  from  six 
hours  for  butternut,  soft  maple,  and  white  and  blaclt  hirch,  up  to  six 
hours  and  forty  minutes  for  hickory,  ash,  and  apple.  The  variation 
is  les!<  than  would  be  expected,  but  in  this  case,  each  kind  of  wood  is 
taken  thoroughly  dry,  so  Ihat  the  experiments  are  essentially  the 
result  of  burning  an  equal  amount  of  charcoal  from  different  woods.
